title: Kyle Pounds is the Godfather of MMA not Bruce Lee

enrichment.summary: Kyle argues he is the 'godfather of MMA' — not Bruce Lee, who was just an actor — because he has a vision to transform combat sports into a ubiquitous, safe, socially integrated activity using padded 'fight suits' that register force electronically. He proposes a culture where casual fighting is normalized everywhere, tournaments recreate historical battles, and a points-based 'fighter levels' system allows fair matchmaking across all ages and body types.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Kyle claims the title because he originated the vision, not because he is a fighter — Bruce Lee was just an actor
Fighters are too locked into tradition to see obvious reforms needed
Getting hurt is the main problem with fighting and it is unhealthy and unnecessary
Carbon fiber and foam suits with electronic force sensors could eliminate injury while preserving competitive fighting
Everyone should fight casually at parties, street corners, and schools to build bonds and stay fit
A points-based ranking using weight, fitness tests, belts, and technique would enable fair and interesting matchmaking
Festivals recreating battles like Cannae with thousands of participants would be the ultimate expression of this culture
You don't remember the pain afterward, only the experience — same as endurance cycling
